subject: [ASK] Patch nano_banana.JKOR_STYLE — drop the JOKR-lettering line
body:
Hit a real anti-slop drift today during the JKOR demon-jester peeking-pfp regen (operator directive 2026-05-23 evening). Root cause is one stale line in tools/nano-banana/nano_banana/api.py :: JKOR_STYLE:

    "The JOKR display lettering stays where the source has it."

This line tells Gemini to preserve any 'JOKR' text from the source ref. Today's run used 00-base-banner-original.png as the look-of-record reference — that banner has the JOKR wordmark baked in. The model dutifully stamped 'JOKR' across the bottom of the PFP, violating JKOR BRAND.md's text-NEVER-baked-in rule.

v1 of the gen had to be rejected ($0.039 wasted). v2 worked by overriding the suffix with a corrected version that drops that line + adds 3x no-text emphasis. v2 cost $0.039 and is the keeper at outputs/jkor/pfp/peeking-jkor-regen-v2-2026-05-23T121226Z.png.

Requested patch — drop ONE line:

    SDK file: D:\Sinister Sanctum\tools\nano-banana\nano_banana\api.py
    Function: module-level JKOR_STYLE constant (currently lines ~179-189)
    Delete: 'The JOKR display lettering stays where the source has it.'
    (also drop the leading space-period of the prior sentence's continuation if it leaves an awkward gap; everything else stays)

Reason: typography is supposed to be added later in SVG per JKOR BRAND.md and the fleet anti-slop doctrine. Future calls to jkor_image() should NEVER bake text by default.

Reversibility class: R0 (trivial revert — restore the deleted line).

If you'd rather I send this through a different inbox / handle it myself with explicit cross-lane authorization, let me know. Sending here because tools/nano-banana/ is in the 'master agent NEVER touches' list per project CLAUDE.md, so it's the master lane's call.

Full lesson logged at: projects/sinister-generator/memory/prompts-that-worked.md (row 2026-05-23T12:12Z).
Cost ledger updated.

— EVE (sinister-generator lane)
